title: fix: clamp text seed to int32 max
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Clamps `seed` in the chat completions schema to signed INT32 max (`2147483647`) — oversized client seeds were reaching strict providers and coming back as 500s, polluting model health stats instead of failing as clean 400s
- Adds schema tests for the INT32 boundary
- Adds a "Low sample" chip to the model monitor when health status is based on fewer than 10 requests
- Documents the oversized-seed failure mode and the `request_inputs` column of `recent_server_errors` in the model-debugging skill

Salvaged from local work left over after #12295 merged.

gist.headline: Taming the giant seeds
gist.blurb: We've clamped text generation seeds to the INT32 maximum to prevent oversized numbers from causing provider errors. Plus, the model monitor now flags low-sample health stats!
gist.summary: Clamps the chat completion `seed` parameter to the signed INT32 maximum (2147483647) to prevent oversized client seeds from triggering 500 errors from strict providers.
gist.impact: API requests with excessively large seeds will now be automatically clamped instead of failing, and the model monitor now displays a 'Low sample' chip for models with fewer than 10 requests.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee trimming a giant glowing digital seed down to a manageable size, while Nomnom eats the excess. A retro monitor in the background shows a 'Low sample' warning.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot using tiny pixelated clippers to trim a giant glowing digital seed down to size, while the round tan Nomnom creature happily eats the trimmed pieces. A retro CRT monitor in the background displays a green 'Low sample' chip. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
